column1: # Graph Offensive — CUDA-graph capture via torch.compile mode=reduce-overhead / max-autotune.
# Every row below explicitly sets them to 0 so compile actually runs. Row 00 leaves them at
# default to *document* the eager-baseline number for comparison.
# 500 iters is enough to see steady-state tok/s after compile warmup and still exercise
# the full eval chain (pre-quant, GPTQ, quantized eval, sliding, TTT).
# TORCH_LOGS=recompiles,graph_breaks emits diagnostics to stderr → captured into the log
# file via run_experiment.sh's `2>&1`. scripts/graph_probe.py parses them.
#   TORCH_COMPILE_MODE_TTT   (TTT wrap,       line 343, role=ttt)  — left at default; Phase 5 only
# Seed pinned to 42 so bpb deltas are measurement (not seed) noise.
# Interleaved default-mode controls catch machine drift.

column2: QK_GAIN_INIT=5.5 WARMDOWN_FRAC=0.64 TTT_ENABLED=1 SLIDING_WINDOW_ENABLED=1 TTT_EPOCHS=1 EMA_DECAY=0.995 LOGIT_SOFTCAP=20 MATRIX_LR=0.042 ITERATIONS=500 MAX_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS=1800 TIMEOUT_SECS=3600 FAST_SMOKE=0 SEED=42 TORCH_LOGS=recompiles,graph_breaks NOTES=eager_compile_disabled
